evals.expected_output: Security review identifying SQL injection in login and profile, plaintext password comparison, XSS in profile template, hardcoded secret key.

assertions.text: Review identifies SQL injection vulnerability in the login() function
Review identifies SQL injection vulnerability in the profile() function
Review flags that passwords are compared as plaintext (not hashed)
Review flags the hardcoded app.secret_key value as a security risk
Review identifies XSS risk in the f-string HTML template in profile()
Review provides at least one concrete code-level fix suggestion (not just general advice)
Findings are labeled with severity levels (critical/high/medium/low or equivalent)

evals.expected_output: Full review: SQL injection (security/critical), N+1 queries (performance), god-function processUserData (architecture), dead code legacyFormatUser (maintainability), mixed callback+async (coding standards).

assertions.text: Review identifies SQL injection vulnerabilities in the JavaScript code
Review identifies the N+1 query pattern in getUsersWithPosts
Review flags processUserData as doing too much / violating single responsibility
Review flags legacyFormatUser as dead code that should be removed
Review flags the mixed use of callbacks and async/await in the same function
Review covers findings across at least 3 distinct domains (security, performance, architecture, maintainability, coding standards)
Review ends with a structured summary table showing severity counts

evals.expected_output: Performance: O(n²) find_duplicates, config read per call. Maintainability: redundant total2 loop, dead legacy branch, avg computed in wrong scope, missing type hints.

assertions.text: Review identifies O(n²) complexity in find_duplicates due to the nested loop
Review flags that config.json is read from disk on every process_report call
Review identifies the redundant total2 loop in calculate_stats (computes same sum as total, result is never used)
Review flags the `if report_type == 'legacy': pass` dead code block
Review catches that avg is computed inside the for loop body (indented too far), causing it to recompute on every non-active item
Review does not raise security or architecture findings (correctly scoped to performance and maintainability only)
